Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your project data is accessible, secure, and connected to your other business systems. Singapore SMEs must comply with PDPA regulations and protect client information, while your team needs to access projects from the office, site, or home on any device. Without this foundation, you risk data breaches, compliance violations, and siloed information that slows decision-making.

Project Core.narrative: This family gives you the core tools to plan, assign, and track work—from breaking down a project into tasks to reminding the team of deadlines and keeping documents in one place. For a Singapore SME juggling multiple clients or internal projects, this prevents tasks from falling through the cracks and keeps everyone aligned on what's due and who's responsible. A construction firm using templates to standardise site setup, or a consulting firm tracking deliverables for each client engagement, both rely on this daily.

Time & Resources.narrative: This family tracks who is working on what and how much it costs, so you know if your team is overloaded, underutilised, or spending more than budgeted. For a Singapore SME billing clients by the hour or managing tight project margins, accurate time and expense capture directly affects invoicing accuracy and profitability. A design agency tracking billable hours per client, or a maintenance contractor logging travel expenses, both need this to stay profitable.

Financial Control.narrative: This family links project spending to budgets and turns project data into client invoices, so you know if a project is making or losing money before it ends. Singapore SMEs operating on fixed-price or time-and-materials contracts depend on this to avoid cost overruns and ensure timely, accurate billing. A software development firm invoicing on milestones, or a consulting firm billing by the hour with expense reimbursement, both need visibility into project profitability.

Delivery & Tracking.narrative: This family gives you a visual timeline of your project, alerts you to risks and delays, and lets customers see progress and approve work without constant emails. For a Singapore SME managing client expectations and delivering on time, a Gantt chart and risk register help you spot problems early, while a customer portal reduces back-and-forth and builds confidence. A renovation contractor showing clients progress photos and timelines, or a software vendor getting sign-off on milestones, both benefit from this transparency.

Analytics & Reporting.narrative: This family turns project data into dashboards and reports so you and your leadership can see at a glance which projects are on track, which are at risk, and how the team is performing. For a Singapore SME with multiple projects or ambitions to grow, regular visibility into project health and team utilisation helps you make faster, smarter decisions about resource allocation and pricing. A project manager reviewing weekly status for the board, or a director spotting which project types are most profitable, both rely on this.
